modules.title: HTML Foundations & Semantic Markup

sections.heading: HTML Document Structure
sections.content: Every HTML page follows a standard structure. DOCTYPE tells browser to use HTML5. html wraps everything. head has metadata. body has visible content. Understanding this is fundamental — interviewers expect you to write it from memory.
sections.code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <meta name="description" content="Job search app">
    <title>JobApp</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
</head>
<body>
    <header><nav>Navigation</nav></header>
    <main><article>Content</article></main>
    <footer>Footer</footer>
    <script src="app.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
sections.tip: CSS in head (loads before render). JS before closing body (loads after content). This prevents render-blocking.

sections.heading: Semantic HTML
sections.content: Semantic tags describe WHAT content is, not how it looks. Screen readers use them for accessibility. Search engines use them for SEO. Using div for everything is the #1 sign of a junior developer.
sections.code:
...
  • Home

Blog Post

Related

© 2024
sections.tip: Semantic HTML improves accessibility, SEO, and readability. Interviewers always ask about this.

sections.heading: Forms — Complete Guide
sections.content: Forms collect user input. Every web app has forms. Understanding elements, validation, and accessibility is essential.
sections.code:
<form action="/api/signup" method="POST">
    <label for="name">Name *</label>
    <input type="text" id="name" name="name" required
           minlength="2" maxlength="100" autocomplete="name">
    
    <label for="email">Email *</label>
    <input type="email" id="email" name="email" required>
    
    <label for="password">Password *</label>
    <input type="password" id="password" name="password"
           required minlength="8"
           pattern="(?=.*\d)(?=.*[a-z])(?=.*[A-Z]).{8,}">
    
    <select id="role" name="role">
        <option value="">Select...</option>
        <option value="dev">Developer</option>
    </select>
    
    <fieldset>
        <legend>Level</legend>
        <label><input type="radio" name="level" value="fresher"> Fresher</label>
        <label><input type="radio" name="level" value="senior"> Senior</label>
    </fieldset>
    
    <label><input type="checkbox" name="terms" required> I agree</label>
    
    <textarea id="bio" rows="4" maxlength="500"></textarea>
    <input type="file" accept=".pdf,.doc">
    
    <button type="submit">Submit</button>
</form>
sections.tip: Always link label to input using for=id. Clicking label focuses input — critical for accessibility and mobile.

sections.heading: Tables and Lists
sections.content: Tables for tabular data only. Lists for navigation and ordered/unordered content.
sections.code:
Salary Report
NameSalary
Alice12.5 LPA
Average11.25 LPA
  • Unordered item
  1. Ordered step
CTC
Cost to Company
sections.tip: scope=col on th helps screen readers. caption describes the table. These show accessibility awareness.

key_points: DOCTYPE html triggers HTML5 standards mode
Only ONE main tag per page
Always link label to input with for=id
Tables for data never for layout

module_quiz.question: meta viewport needed for?
module_quiz.explanation: Without it mobile browsers zoom out to fit desktop layout.

module_quiz.question: main tag how many per page?
module_quiz.explanation: One main element representing primary content.

module_quiz.question: label for='email' links to?
module_quiz.explanation: for matches id attribute of the input element.

module_quiz.question: div for everything is?
module_quiz.explanation: Divs have no meaning. Screen readers and SEO cant understand structure.

module_quiz.question: script tag best placed?
module_quiz.explanation: Before body close doesnt block HTML parsing.

modules.title: CSS Fundamentals & Box Model

sections.heading: CSS Selectors and Specificity
sections.content: Selectors target elements. Specificity determines which styles win on conflicts. Understanding specificity is tested in every interview.
sections.code:
/* Selectors */
p { }                  /* Element */
.card { }              /* Class — most common */
#header { }            /* ID — highest specificity */
input[type="email"] { } /* Attribute */
.card > p { }          /* Direct child */
.card p { }            /* Any descendant */
.card + p { }          /* Adjacent sibling */
a:hover { }            /* Pseudo-class */
p::first-line { }      /* Pseudo-element */

/* SPECIFICITY: inline > ID > class > element */
/* (1,0,0) ID beats (0,10,0) ten classes! */
#nav .link { color: blue; }     /* (1,1,0) = wins */
.nav .link.active { color: red; } /* (0,3,0) = loses */
sections.tip: Avoid ID selectors in CSS. They are too specific to override. Use classes for everything.

sections.heading: Box Model
sections.content: Every element is a box: content + padding + border + margin. box-sizing: border-box makes width include padding and border.
sections.tip: First line of every CSS file: *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }

sections.heading: Units
sections.content: px is fixed. rem is relative to root (accessible). em compounds through nesting. Viewport units relative to screen.
sections.tip: Use rem for fonts — respects user browser settings for accessibility. px for borders only.

key_points: rem for fonts px for borders

module_quiz.question: #nav .link vs .nav .link.active?
module_quiz.explanation: ID (1,1,0) always beats class-only (0,3,0).

module_quiz.question: box-sizing: border-box?
module_quiz.explanation: Total width = set width with padding and border inside.

module_quiz.question: 1rem equals?
module_quiz.explanation: rem = root em. Relative to html font-size.

module_quiz.question: Margin collapse?
options: Adjacent vertical margins combine larger wins
module_quiz.explanation: 20px + 30px = 30px gap not 50px.

module_quiz.question: Best font-size unit?
module_quiz.explanation: rem respects browser font-size for accessibility.

modules.title: Flexbox Layout

sections.heading: Flexbox Complete
sections.content: One-dimensional layout. Handles row OR column. Most used for components like navbars, cards, centering.
sections.tip: justify-content = main axis. align-items = cross axis. If direction is column they SWAP.

sections.heading: Flexbox Patterns
sections.content: Common real-world patterns.
sections.tip: margin-left: auto on flex child pushes it to the right. Very useful for navbars.

key_points: Flexbox is one-dimensional row OR column

module_quiz.question: justify-content: space-between?
options: First last at edges equal gaps between
module_quiz.explanation: First at start last at end equal gaps between.

module_quiz.question: align-items axis?
module_quiz.explanation: Cross axis. In row direction thats vertical.

module_quiz.question: flex: 1?
module_quiz.explanation: flex-grow:1 flex-shrink:1 flex-basis:0.

module_quiz.question: Center vertically and horizontally?
module_quiz.explanation: Three flex properties for perfect centering.

module_quiz.question: flex-wrap: wrap?
module_quiz.explanation: Items move to next line when no space.

modules.title: CSS Grid Layout

sections.heading: Grid Fundamentals
sections.content: Two-dimensional layout handling rows AND columns. Most powerful CSS layout system.
sections.tip: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)) creates responsive grid without media queries.

sections.heading: Grid vs Flexbox
sections.content: Use both together. Grid for page layout. Flexbox for component internals.
sections.tip: Grid for macro layout. Flexbox for micro layout. Most projects use both.

key_points: Grid is two-dimensional rows AND columns
Grid for page layout Flexbox for components

module_quiz.question: Grid vs Flexbox?
module_quiz.explanation: Grid handles both dimensions. Flexbox one direction.

module_quiz.question: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr))?
module_quiz.explanation: Auto columns at least 250px growing to fill. No media queries.

module_quiz.question: grid-column: 1 / -1?
module_quiz.explanation: -1 means last grid line. Spans from first to last.

module_quiz.question: fr unit?
module_quiz.explanation: 1fr 2fr: second twice as wide as first.

module_quiz.question: grid-template-areas purpose?
module_quiz.explanation: Visual placement with named grid regions.

modules.title: Responsive Design

sections.heading: Mobile-First Design
sections.content: Design for mobile first then add for larger screens with min-width. Over 70% of Indian internet is mobile.
sections.tip: Mobile-first uses min-width queries. clamp() eliminates multiple font-size breakpoints.

sections.heading: Responsive Patterns
sections.content: Common patterns for production responsive design.
sections.tip: Container queries respond to parent size not viewport. Future of responsive design.

key_points: Mobile-first base CSS then min-width for larger

module_quiz.question: Mobile-first uses?
module_quiz.explanation: min-width = this size AND up. Base is mobile.

module_quiz.question: img max-width 100%?
module_quiz.explanation: Never wider than container but doesnt scale up beyond natural.

module_quiz.question: clamp(1rem, 4vw, 3rem)?
module_quiz.explanation: Scales fluidly between min and max based on viewport.

module_quiz.question: Container queries respond to?
module_quiz.explanation: Check parent element size not viewport.

module_quiz.question: aspect-ratio: 16/9?
module_quiz.explanation: Auto maintains width-to-height ratio.

modules.title: Interview Questions

sections.heading: CSS Specificity
sections.content: Multiple rules same element — specificity decides winner.
sections.tip: Never use !important except to override third-party library styles.

sections.heading: Position Property
sections.content: static (default), relative (offset from normal), absolute (removed from flow), fixed (stays on scroll), sticky (hybrid).
sections.tip: position absolute without positioned ancestor positions relative to body.

sections.heading: Display and Visibility
sections.content: display:none removes from flow. visibility:hidden hides but keeps space. opacity:0 transparent but interactive.
sections.tip: For accessibility use .sr-only to hide visually but keep for screen readers.

key_points: sticky is hybrid relative and fixed
display none vs visibility hidden vs opacity 0

module_quiz.question: !important should be?
module_quiz.explanation: Overusing makes CSS unmaintainable.

module_quiz.question: position absolute relative to?
module_quiz.explanation: Looks up for relative/absolute/fixed ancestor. Falls back to body.

module_quiz.question: display none vs visibility hidden?
module_quiz.explanation: none = gone. hidden = invisible but space occupied.

module_quiz.question: z-index works on?
module_quiz.explanation: Needs position other than static.

module_quiz.question: position sticky?
options: Normal then sticks at scroll point
module_quiz.explanation: Relative until scroll position then acts fixed.

modules.title: Animations & Transitions

sections.heading: Transitions and Keyframes
sections.content: Transitions animate between two states on trigger. Keyframes create multi-step animations that can loop.
sections.tip: Only animate transform and opacity. Others cause expensive layout recalculation every frame.

key_points: Transitions need triggers animations run automatically
forwards keeps final state after animation

module_quiz.question: Best to animate?
module_quiz.explanation: GPU accelerated no layout recalculation.

module_quiz.question: Transition needs?
options: Trigger like hover or class change
module_quiz.explanation: Transitions animate between two states on change.

module_quiz.question: animation forwards?
module_quiz.explanation: Without forwards snaps back to original.

module_quiz.question: prefers-reduced-motion for?
module_quiz.explanation: Respects OS setting for motion sensitive users.

module_quiz.question: animation infinite?
module_quiz.explanation: Repeats endlessly.

sections.heading: CSS Variables and Dark Mode
sections.content: CSS variables make theming easy. Dark mode becomes just swapping variable values.
sections.tip: CSS variables cascade. Setting on parent changes for all children. Dark mode = just swap values.

sections.heading: Modern CSS Features
sections.content: New features reducing need for JavaScript.
sections.tip: :has() is the most powerful CSS selector ever. Style parents based on children.

module_quiz.question: CSS variable on :root is?
module_quiz.explanation: Cascades to all descendants.

module_quiz.question: Dark mode with variables?
options: Change variable values on theme selector
module_quiz.explanation: Different values for dark theme. All rules auto update.

module_quiz.question: :has(.badge) selects?
module_quiz.explanation: Parent selector. Selects elements with matching descendants.

module_quiz.question: scroll-snap-type?
module_quiz.explanation: Items snap into position when scrolling.

module_quiz.question: accent-color?
module_quiz.explanation: One property themes native form controls.

modules.title: CSS Architecture & BEM

sections.heading: BEM Naming
sections.content: Block Element Modifier. Most popular CSS naming system. Makes CSS predictable and maintainable.
sections.tip: BEM looks verbose but prevents specificity problems. Every selector is single class.

sections.heading: CSS Organization
sections.content: Structure files by purpose for large projects.
sections.tip: base → components → layouts → utilities. Import order determines cascade.

key_points: Single class selectors no specificity wars

module_quiz.question: BEM .card__title means?
options: title is ELEMENT of card block
module_quiz.explanation: Double underscore separates block from element.

module_quiz.question: BEM .card--featured means?
module_quiz.explanation: Double dash separates block from modifier.

module_quiz.question: BEM advantage?
options: No specificity conflicts all single class
module_quiz.explanation: Single class selectors. No nesting needed no !important.

module_quiz.question: Utility .mt-2?
module_quiz.explanation: Single purpose class for one specific style.

module_quiz.question: CSS file order?
module_quiz.explanation: Base first utilities last for proper cascade.

modules.title: Accessibility & Performance

sections.heading: Web Accessibility
sections.content: Make websites work for everyone including disabilities. WCAG is the standard. Screen readers keyboard navigation and color contrast are pillars.
sections.code:
Success!
Clickable div
Skip to content /* Focus visible */ :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #C89E53; outline-offset: 2px; } /* NEVER: *:focus { outline: none; } */ /* Contrast: WCAG AA normal text 4.5:1 */ /* Large text (18px+ bold or 24px+): 3:1 */ /* Reduced motion */ @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { * { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; } } .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); }
sections.tip: Never remove focus outline. Use :focus-visible for keyboard only. This single practice shows accessibility awareness.

sections.heading: CSS Performance
sections.content: Optimize for fast loading and smooth rendering.
sections.tip: content-visibility: auto is biggest CSS performance win. Skips off-screen rendering.

key_points: Alt text for meaningful images empty for decorative
will-change hints GPU remove after use

module_quiz.question: alt='' means?
module_quiz.explanation: Empty alt = decorative. Missing alt is violation.

module_quiz.question: :focus-visible vs :focus?
module_quiz.explanation: Shows outline for keyboard users not mouse clicks.

module_quiz.question: WCAG AA contrast normal text?
module_quiz.explanation: Normal 4.5:1. Large 3:1. AAA 7:1.

module_quiz.question: content-visibility auto?
module_quiz.explanation: Browser doesnt render until approaching viewport.

module_quiz.question: Never remove focus outline because?
module_quiz.explanation: Essential for keyboard navigation accessibility.

quiz.question: justify vs align?
quiz.explanation: justify main axis align cross axis

quiz.question: transition vs animation?
quiz.explanation: Transition needs hover. Animation auto plays.

quiz.question: none vs hidden?
quiz.explanation: none gone hidden invisible space kept

quiz.question: em vs rem?
quiz.explanation: em nests rem stays root relative

quiz.question: BEM .card__title--bold?
options: title element bold modifier of card

quiz.question: Native CSS nesting?
quiz.explanation: Native nesting support in modern browsers

quiz.question: alt for decorative?
quiz.explanation: Empty tells screen reader to skip
